Senator lilorgan may transfer any funds currently held in her
rrFriends of Becky llorganrt controlled cornrnittee to her controlled

ANALYSIS
California voters approved Proposition 73 on June 7, 1988.
Proposition 73 anended the Act by establishing limits on
contributions to candidates and their controlled connittees.
Proposition 73 also added Section 85305 which states:
tl 
Government Code Section 81000-91015. AII statutory references
Ete to the Governrnent Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 caLifornia code of Regulations Section
18000, et ses. AII references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the CaIj-fornia Code of Regulations.

Kelly Barrales
Page 2
Any person who possesses campaign funds on the effective date
of- this- chapter uray expend these funds for any larrful purfose
other than to support or oppose a candidacy for electile '
office.
On September 14,
the Eastern District
1989, the United States District Court for
of California declared Section 85306 invalid.
(Fair PoIi!icaI practiceq Cornmission, No. cIV. s-89-433 LKK.)
As a result of this ruling, the term .restricted fund.s. no ionger
applies to campaign funds received prior to January L, 1989, anal
such funds may no!, be used to support or oppose a candidacy for
elective office. 
However, candidates and otficenolders wh-o
possess those funds may not use qgy campaj,gn funds, including
funds received prior to January r., 1989, to rnake contributions to
support or oppose any other candidate for elective office.
(Section 85304. )
AlL expenditures made and contributions received during a
reporting period rnust be reported on a cornmittee,s campaign
statement. (Section 84211.) Therefore, when reportin; a transfer
of funds anong a candidate,s own controlled corntn-ittees I the
connittee naking the transfer of funds should report the
transaction as an expenditure on schedure E of the Form 490 ancl
the conrrrittee receiving the funds should report it as an increase
to cash on Schedule c of the FonD 49O.
If you have additional questions, please contact ne at (916)
322-5662.
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
ceneral Counsel
